symptoms.symptom_name: Exquisite, focal anterior neck pain, tender to light touch, worse on swallowing/head-turning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painful, tender thyroid is the hallmark distinguishing subacute thyroiditis from painless thyrotoxic causes.
The Graves gland is characteristically non-tender and painless.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain radiating to jaw, ears and occiput

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic radiation pattern of subacute thyroiditis pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding upper-respiratory viral illness ~3 weeks earlier

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The post-viral granulomatous mechanism is central to subacute thyroiditis and helps distinguish it from bacterial thyroiditis and Graves disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations, heat intolerance and fine hand tremor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenergic thyrotoxic symptoms from unregulated release of stored hormone.
On a febrile, tachycardic background these features mandate screening for decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fever with drenching sweats and systemic malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever with a painful neck cannot be assumed viral — bacterial infection must be excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe odynophagia with reduced oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep neck space infection / retropharyngeal abscess
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Odynophagia with fever is a deep-space-infection warning that must be actively excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Airway intact — no stridor, drooling, voice change or breathing difficulty

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep neck space infection / retropharyngeal abscess
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patent airway with normal voice lowers immediate airway-threat probability but does not exclude a deep-space infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Enlarged, firm, exquisitely tender thyroid gland on palpation; no overlying erythema or fluctuance; absent cervical lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender diffuse gland without abscess signs supports granulomatous over bacterial thyroiditis — a clinician exam finding, sealed and unavailable via telehealth.
Absent erythema/fluctuance and lymphadenopathy argue against abscess — but this determination requires in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suppressed TSH with raised free T4/free T3

investigation_details.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3)
investigation_details.expected_result: Suppressed TSH with elevated free T4/T3 (thyrotoxic phase)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Thyrotoxicosis and its severity go unquantified; storm risk unassessed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the biochemical thyrotoxic phase — a laboratory result unobtainable via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated ESR (classic in subacute thyroiditis) with raised CRP
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The inflammatory burden and bacterial-vs-granulomatous distinction remain unresolved.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated ESR is characteristic; a laboratory result unobtainable via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Low/suppressed uptake (destructive thyroiditis rather than Graves overproduction)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Distinguishing destructive thyroiditis from Graves may remain uncertain if bloods are equivocal.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suppressed uptake distinguishes destructive thyroiditis from Graves — a nuclear-medicine investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Painful tender anterior neck radiating to jaw/ears after a viral prodrome plus thyrotoxic features is the characteristic cluster.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies thyrotoxicosis and informs storm risk — arranged same-day but performed in person/at pathology, not via telehealth.
Quantifies inflammation and helps distinguish granulomatous from bacterial thyroiditis.
Palpation and inspection for erythema/fluctuance/lymphadenopathy are required to exclude abscess/deep-space infection — cannot be done via telehealth.
Assesses gland and excludes abscess/collection if diagnosis remains uncertain.
Only if the diagnosis remains uncertain after bloods; not an acute telehealth-orderable test.
Not for the AI to order remotely; reserved for the in-person clinician if a deep neck space infection/abscess is suspected on examination.
